notes: This path keeps selected layer weights resident as dequantized f32 runtime buffers; full-model residency may exceed current GPU memory before sq-format work.

Decode linear-attention layer_step_ms can shift deferred GPU work to later host-boundary layers when consecutive linear-attention layers stay device-resident; use layers_step_ms and step_wall_summary for throughput comparisons.

text_prompt.raw: Explain in one concise paragraph why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements.
text_prompt.rendered: Explain in one concise paragraph why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements.

decoded_text.prompt: Explain in one concise paragraph why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements.
decoded_text.generated: GPU warmup distorts token/s measurements because the initial frames of inference are dominated by the overhead of loading model weights into high-bandwidth memory and initializing CUDA kernels, rather than pure computation; consequently, the GPU operates below its peak throughput during this period, causing the calculated tokens-per-second metric—which divides total tokens by total elapsed time—to be artificially depressed until the hardware reaches thermal and power equilibrium.
decoded_text.full:
Explain in one concise paragraph why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements.

GPU warmup distorts token/s measurements because the initial frames of inference are dominated by the overhead of loading model weights into high-bandwidth memory and initializing CUDA kernels, rather than pure computation; consequently, the GPU operates below its peak throughput during this period, causing the calculated tokens-per-second metric—which divides total tokens by total elapsed time—to be artificially depressed until the hardware reaches thermal and power equilibrium.
